DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-07-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS DC DESIGN & CONSTRUCTION SERVICES, INC, DAVID CHANG and PAOLIN CHANG'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. The Demurrer is overruled on the grounds of exclusive concurrent jurisdiction, res judicata, and collateral estoppel, as none of these doctrines bar this action. First, both actions are pending in the same superior court and the Court in the action brought by Defendant for administrative mandamus lacks the power to litigate all of the issues and grant all of the relief sought in this action. Second, there has been no final judgment issued in the other pending action. The Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the allegations supporting a theory of alter ego liability. Plaintiff concedes that the First Amended Complaint (FAC) fails to allege an unjust result, which is a required element for alter ego liability, and the cases relied upon by Plaintiff were presented in a different procedural posture than this case and are not compelling authority here. When the court is asked to take some action upon an alter ego theory at the pleadings stage, more is required than was pleaded here. (See, e.g., Norins Realty Co. v. Consolidated Abstract & Title Guaranty Co. (1947) 80 Cal.App.2d 879.) The Demurrer is sustained as to the breach of contract cause of action. While the FAC sufficiently states the elements of the cause of action, in light of the failure to sufficiently allege alter ego liability, Plaintiff has not alleged a cause of action against the individual defendants. Plaintiff has 10 days leave to amend to do so. The Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the cause of action for negligence. The FAC fails to state a claim distinct from the breach of contract claim. Next, the Demurrer is sustained as to the cause of action for negligent misrepresentation. Plaintiff concedes that the FAC fails to allege required elements. Additionally, justifiable reliance must be specifically pled. Plaintiff has 10 days leave to amend. Finally, the Demurrer is sustained as to the intentional misrepresentation. Plaintiff has 10 days leave to amend in order to allege where Defendant David Chang made the alleged misrepresentations; and how, where, to whom and by what means the alleged misrepresentations were made by Defendant Paolin Chang.
ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg
